title: Getting Data
text: vignette first-time users recorderFeedback. explains recipient data observation data loaded project way can repeated every reporting cycle.

title: Getting Data
text: : project created rf_init(). config.yml file project root. package loaded library(recorderFeedback). initialised project yet, run: work inside folder relative paths config.yml resolve correctly.

title: Getting Data
text: Data input controlled project’s config.yml. important fields : recipients_file: Path recipients data (e.g., "data/recipients.csv"). recipients_script: Script loading recipients (e.g., "scripts/get_recipients.R"). data_file: Path records/observations data (e.g., "data/data.csv"). data_script: Script loading records (e.g., "scripts/get_data.R"). *_script create update corresponding *_file.

title: Getting Data
text: rf_get_recipients() runs script defined recipients_script. script write recipients table recipients_file. minimum, recipients data include: recipient_id name email

title: Getting Data
text: rf_get_data() runs script defined data_script. script write raw records data_file. records must include recipient_id record can linked recipient.

title: Getting Data
text: Run validation immediately loading data. catches missing files, missing columns, recipient/data mismatches rendering.

title: Getting Data
text: Data file updated: scripts/get_data.R script write config$data_file. Recipient file updated: scripts/get_recipients.R script write config$recipients_file. Missing column errors: ensure required columns named exactly (recipient_id, name, email).

title: Getting Data
text: data loading works, continue “Generating Content” vignette render recipient-specific reports.

title: Generating Content
text: vignette explains turn loaded data personalised feedback pages. aimed new users already completed “Getting Data” steps.

title: Generating Content
text: content stage three parts: Optional computations focal background data. Rendering one recipient testing. Rendering recipients batch.

title: Generating Content
text: Content generation controlled config.yml. Key entries : computation_script_focal: Script computations focal (recipient-specific) data. computation_script_bg: Script background computations. content_template_file: RMarkdown template rendering feedback. html_template_file: HTML template styling output. default, computation script fields point "scripts/computation.R".

title: Generating Content
text: Computation scripts let prepare summaries metrics rendering. useful totals, rankings, trends, values want reuse templates. normal use, computations executed automatically part rendering. building custom scripts, core helper used internally: computation script define compute_objects(records_data) return object (often list) can used templates.

title: Generating Content
text: Start rendering single recipient check template logic running full batch. run whole batch:
code: # Use a known recipient_id from recipients_file rf_render_single(recipient_id = 1) batch_id <- "example_batch" rf_render_all(batch_id)

title: Generating Content
text: templates/content.Rmd: Defines content structure computed objects shown. templates/template.html: Controls final page styling layout wrapper. Keep template edits small test rf_render_single() change.

title: Generating Content
text: generation, can view verify content:

title: Generating Content
text: meta_table.csv missing: batch rendering complete. Template errors render: test rf_render_single() isolate failing recipient. Missing recipient output: check recipient_id values match recipients data files.

title: Generating Content
text: content renders cleanly, continue “Distributing Content” vignette send output email.

title: Distributing Content
text: vignette explains safely send generated feedback email. designed new users already rendered batch successfully.

title: Distributing Content
text: Confirm prerequisites first: run rf_render_all(batch_id). renders/<batch_id>/meta_table.csv exists. rf_verify_batch(batch_id) completes without blocking errors.

title: Distributing Content
text: Distribution settings managed project’s config.yml file. Key entries include: mail_server: SMTP server address. mail_port: SMTP port (default: 587). mail_use_tls / mail_use_ssl: Security options email. mail_username, mail_password: Credentials authentication. mail_sender, mail_name: Sender details. mail_subject: Subject line feedback emails. mail_test_recipient: Address test emails. mail_creds: Authentication mode ("anonymous" "envvar"). test_mode: TRUE, messages redirected mail_test_recipient.

title: Distributing Content
text: generating feedback content, use rf_dispatch_smtp() send emails recipients. function uses configuration connect mail server distribute feedback. safe first run, keep test_mode: TRUE config.yml. sends outputs mail_test_recipient instead real recipient addresses.

title: Distributing Content
text: Email body content comes content_template_file html_template_file. Sending format controlled email_format (usually templates/email_format.R).

title: Distributing Content
text: dispatch, can verify emails sent check errors: also inspect status report email sent mail_test_recipient.

title: Distributing Content
text: Authentication failure: review mail_creds, username/password, host, port. TLS/SSL mismatch: check mail_use_tls mail_use_ssl SMTP provider. Footer email mismatch error: generated file email match meta table row.

title: Distributing Content
text: Email distribution fully configurable via config.yml. Use rf_dispatch_smtp() send feedback recipients. Keep test_mode: TRUE dry runs, switch FALSE live sending. approach helps validate delivery safely contacting real recipients.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: end--end example shows use recorderFeedback iNaturalist data. written new users, stage explicit: initialise project, load recipients, fetch observations, render content, distribute.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: end : recipients table linked iNaturalist user IDs. records table fetched iNaturalist API. simple personalised report template. One test render one batch render. Optional SMTP dispatch.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: Initialise project directory read config: Run subsequent commands project directory relative paths config.yml valid.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: Recipients individuals feedback reports generated. example, manually define script. File: scripts/get_recipients.R Run recipient script: Inspect recipients:
code: config <- config::get() df <- data.frame(   email = c("simon@example.com"),   name = c("Simon"),   recipient_id = c("1152941") # iNaturalist user ID ) write.csv(df, config$recipients_file, row.names = FALSE) rf_get_recipients() read.csv(config$recipients_file)

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: now pull iNaturalist observations recipient last 30 days. Install required packages needed: File: scripts/get_data.R Run verify:
code: install.packages(c("httr", "jsonlite")) library(httr) library(jsonlite)  get_inat_observations <- function(user_id) {   today <- Sys.Date()   start_date <- today - 30      base_url <- "https://api.inaturalist.org/v1/observations"   query <- list(     user_id = user_id,     d1 = start_date,     d2 = today,     per_page = 30,     order = "desc",     order_by = "created_at"   )      response <- GET(base_url, query = query)   stop_for_status(response)      data <- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")   obs <- fromJSON(data, flatten = TRUE)      output <- obs$results[, c(     "id",     "user.id",     "observed_on",     "taxon.preferred_common_name",     "taxon.name",     "taxon.iconic_taxon_name"   )]   names(output) <- c(     "record_id",     "recipient_id",     "date",     "common_name",     "scientific_name",     "species_group"   )      return(output) }  recipients <- read.csv(config$recipients_file) observations <- data.frame() for (i in seq_len(nrow(recipients))) {   observations <- rbind(observations,                         get_inat_observations(recipients$recipient_id[i])) }  write.csv(observations, config$data_file, row.names = FALSE) rf_get_data() rf_verify_data(verbose = TRUE)

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: build simple R Markdown template generates personalized summary recipient. template includes: Greeting recipient name Count unique species recorded table recent observations (keep vignette self-contained, template text printed directly.)
code: #> --- #>  title: "iNaturalist Summary" #>  params: #>    recipient_name: "" #>    recipient_email: "UNKNOWN" #>    focal_data: "" #>    bg_data: "" #>    focal_computed_objects: "" #>    bg_computed_objects: "" #>    content_key: "" #>    config: "" #>    extra_params: "" #>  footer-date-time: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z')`" #>  recipient-email: "`r params$recipient_email`" #>  --- #>   #>  ```{r setup, include=FALSE} #>  knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, #>                        fig.align = 'center', error = FALSE, results = 'asis') #>  ``` #>   #>  # Hello `r params$recipient_name`! #>   #>  You have recorded `r length(unique(params$focal_data$scientific_name))` species recently! #>   #>  ```{r table} #>  table_data <- params$focal_data[, c('date', 'species_group', 'scientific_name', 'common_name')] #>  names(table_data) <- c('Date', 'Group', 'Species', 'Common name') #>  knitr::kable(table_data) #>  ``` #>   #>  Best wishes,   #>  recorderFeedback

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: Render single report testing: Render recipients:
code: rf_render_single(recipient_id = "1152941") batch_id <- "vignette_demo" rf_render_all(batch_id = batch_id) rf_verify_batch(batch_id, verbose = TRUE)

text: records returned: verify iNaturalist recipient_id values valid user IDs. API errors: rerun later reduce request size testing. Render failure: test rf_render_single() inspect template chunk fails.

title: Example: iNaturalist Summary
text: vignette showed : Define recipients. Fetch iNaturalist records via API. Build personalised R Markdown report. Render reports individually batch. Distribute via email. recorderFeedback package provides structured workflow automating ecological feedback loops iNaturalist activity summaries.

code: @Manual{,   title = {recorderFeedback: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback},   author = {Simon Rolph},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://simonrolph.github.io/recorderFeedback/}, }

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: recorderFeedback R package generating personalised, data-driven feedback, originally designed citizen science wildlife recording adaptable domains. package provides tools set feedback workflow, load data, generate content, distribute feedback recipients.

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: Project Initialisation: Quickly scaffold new feedback project necessary files folders. Data Loading: Import recipient observation data configurable sources. Computation: Run custom analyses summaries data generating feedback. Content Rendering: Create personalised feedback documents recipient using RMarkdown templates. Batch Processing: Automate feedback generation multiple recipients. Email Dispatch: Send feedback via email using SMTP. Package functions prefixed rf_.

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: Install GitHub using devtools:

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: initialise project rf_init(), files copied set workflow, ensuring necessary templates, scripts, configuration get started quickly. config.yml: central configuration file referenced README, set paths options project. scripts/computation.R, scripts/get_data.R, scripts/get_recipients.R: Scripts loading data, recipients, running computations, matching workflow steps outlined README. templates/content.Rmd, templates/email_format.R, templates/template.html: Templates rendering feedback formatting emails run_pipeline.R, _targets.R: Pipeline orchestration scripts, supporting batch processing automation diagram attempts explain fits together: Key terms: Recipient: someone receive feedback Focal/background: whether data relates recipient (focal), (background) Template: RMarkdown document defining data manipulated visualised Computations: calculations processing done raw data prior rendering template

title: Generating personalised, data-driven feedback
text: rendering template fails pipeline continue, won’t .html file designated renders/[batch_id] folder. Using rf_verify_batch() show recipients rendering failed. Using targets::tar_meta(fields = "error") also tell targets failed corresponding error messages. Use render_single(recipient_id) render individual feedback item view detailed error message shown.
